title: Building a Clean Preprocessing Pipeline
concept: A clean preprocessing pipeline coordinates all transformations in the correct order: random changes for training, clean shaping for validation, and standard normalization for both. This sets the foundation for stable CNN training.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the correct way to handle crops in train vs validation pipelines?

options.text: Train uses random resized crops; validation uses deterministic center crops
Train uses center crops; validation uses random crops
Both must use identical random crops to be fair

exercises.explain: Yes! Random crops for train; center crops for validation.

cards.title: Tying It All Together
cards.body: We've covered geometric transforms, photometric color shifts, multi-image blends like Mixup, and mandatory resizing and normalization. Now, it's time to assemble these individual steps into a single, clean pipeline that feeds our CNN.

cards.title: The Complete Pipeline
cards.body: A clean pipeline maps data loading to network execution. During training, raw images are loaded, augmented (geometric + photometric), resized, normalized, and batched. During testing, raw images are simply resized, normalized, and batched.

cards.title: The water purification plant
cards.body: A clean pipeline is like a water purification plant. Muddy water goes through distinct filters in a specific order: large debris removal first (resizing), chemical treatment next (augmentation), and final purification (normalization). If you change the order or skip a filter, the output isn't clean.

cards.title: The PyTorch / TensorFlow Standard
cards.body:
In PyTorch (torchvision) or TensorFlow, you chain transforms together:
transforms.Compose([
transforms.RandomResizedCrop(224),
transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip(),
transforms.ColorJitter(0.2, 0.2),
transforms.ToTensor(),
transforms.Normalize(mean, std)
]).

cards.title: Augmentation happens in memory, not on disk
cards.body: It's a common myth that data augmentation overwrites your image files on disk or permanently saves thousands of new files. Augmentations are applied in **RAM / GPU memory** on the fly as each batch is loaded. Your original image files on disk remain completely untouched.

cards.title: The Complete Preprocessing Pipeline
cards.body: Reviewing the complete end-to-end data flow:

left.text:
Unchanged source files
Various sizes & formats
Persistent storage

right.text:
Randomly augmented (train)
Standardized size (224x224)
Normalized to zero-mean floats

exercises.prompt: Where are data augmentation transformations typically applied and stored?

options.text: In-memory on the fly during batch loading; the original files on disk remain untouched
Permanently saved to disk, replacing the original image files
Inside the weights of the convolutional layers
Only in the cloud database before downloading

exercises.explain: Correct! Augmentation happens dynamically in memory as batches load.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A clean preprocessing pipeline applies normalization as the final step right before the image tensor is passed into the CNN.
exercises.explain: True. Normalization must be the final step to avoid breaking prior transformation math.

exercises.sentence: In PyTorch or TensorFlow, transformations are chained together using a ___ function to process batches in memory.
exercises.explain: Compose (or pipeline) functions chain multiple steps into a single reusable transform pipeline.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary benefit of applying augmentations on the fly in memory rather than saving them to disk?

options.text: It saves disk space and allows infinite random variations without duplicate files
It completely removes the need for a GPU during training
It automatically corrects any labeling errors in the dataset

exercises.explain: Correct! On-the-fly augmentation avoids disk bloat while generating fresh random variations every epoch.

exercises.prompt: We have completed our study of geometric transforms, photometric jitter, image mixing, and preprocessing pipelines. What is the next logical step in training CNNs?

options.text: Handling imbalanced datasets, where some classes have far more images than others
Replacing all convolutional layers with fully connected ones
Converting all images back to JPEG files on disk
Skip training and deploy the model immediately

exercises.explain: Exactly! Now that we have a clean pipeline, we must handle real-world dataset challenges like class imbalance. Next up: Training CNNs in Practice - Wave 2!
